what_it_does: Defines a tiny React MenuButton component: the bottom-centre dark 'MENU' pill for the Virtual Hub. Renders a chevron glyph and the MENU label, toggles an 'open' class based on the open prop, and fires onClick. Registered as window.MenuButton.
observations: Takes two props: open (boolean toggling the 'open' class) and onClick (handler).
Uses a ⌃ unicode chevron span and a plain 'MENU' label.
Attaches to window.MenuButton rather than module export.
conventions: Follows the vh-* class naming and global-window registration patterns of the kit.
Purely presentational, state lifted to parent (controlled via open/onClick).
notes: The bottom-centre MENU toggle pill for the Virtual Hub overlay — controlled, presentational, one-liner.
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the README's row: MenuButton.jsx is the bottom-centre dark MENU pill.
intention_signals: Provides the single entry-point control that reveals the Quick Menu over the immersive viewer.
contribution: Contributes the toggle-pill control pattern (open-state class + parent-controlled click) that opens the Virtual Hub's Quick Menu.
summary_for_embedding: React MenuButton component rendering a bottom-centre dark MENU pill with a chevron and an open-state class for the Virtual Hub.
intention_for_embedding: To give the immersive viewer a single minimal control that reveals the Quick Menu.
